item_a_title: Corporate Bond Risk Falls in Europe, Credit-Default Swaps Show
item_b_title: Corporate Bond Risk Falls in Europe, Credit-Default Swaps Show
item_a_lede:
The cost of insuring against default
on European corporate debt fell, according to traders of credit-
default swaps. Contracts on the Markit iTraxx Crossover Index of 50
companies with mostly high-yield credit ratings dropped 12.5
basis points to 623.5, according to JPMorgan Chase & Co. at 7:30
a.m. in London. A decline signals improved perceptions of credit
quality . The Markit iTraxx Europe Index of 125 companies with
investment-grade ratings was down 2.5 at 147.75 basis points.
The Markit iTraxx Financial Index linked to senior debt of 25
banks and insurers declined six basis points to 221

(GS) vice president sued by regulators over his role in a failed billion-dollar investment, sent three notes with questions to the presiding judge during its second day of deliberations. The jury of four men and five women is considering whether to find Tourre liable for misleading participants in a 2007 transaction known as Abacus. item_b_lede:
Fabrice Tourre, aka “Fabulous Fab,”
was a mere cog in the Wall Street machine that, in 2008,
crippled the global financial system and sent the world economy
into a deep slump. The former Goldman Sachs Group Inc. vice
president’s trial, though, goes to the heart of the lessons we
must learn from that experience. A jury found Tourre liable for misleading investors in a
2007 mortgage deal. Known as Abacus 2007-AC1, the deal allowed
investors to make side bets on the performance of bonds backed
by home loans made to shaky borrowers. At issue was whether

item_a_title: Google Files Complaint in Europe Against Microsoft, Nokia

made last year of some 2,000 patents and patent applications to
Mosaid Technologies Inc., a Canadian company known for its
patent litigation, said Jim Prosser, a spokesman for Google.

technology, and Microsoft has publicly pledged it wouldn’t use

item_b_lede: Even as they battle in courts over smartphone and video technology, Microsoft Corp. (MSFT) and Google Inc.
